prerequisites: No account, API key, or token is required: PokeAPI is a fully open public API and all resources are available without credentials.
Network access to the PokeAPI host at https://pokeapi.co/api/v2/.
Know the exact pokemon_name (or its numeric Pokedex id) you want to extract, since this is the one required config field and maps to the /pokemon/{name} endpoint.
Agree to the PokeAPI fair use policy: cache responses locally and limit request frequency. Per the docs, IPs that ignore fair use can be permanently banned.

authMethods.name: No authentication
authMethods.summary: PokeAPI requires no credentials. The official docs state "No authentication is required to access this API, and all resources are fully open and available." The connector has no secret fields, so you only provide the pokemon_name config value.

setupSteps.title: Choose the Pokemon to extract
setupSteps.body: Decide which Pokemon you want data for and use its lowercase name (for example clefairy) or its numeric Pokedex id. This value is read from the PokeAPI /pokemon/{name} endpoint at https://pokeapi.co/api/v2/.

setupSteps.title: Set the pokemon_name config field
setupSteps.body: Provide the chosen name or id as the required pokemon_name field. This is the only configuration the connector needs; there are no secret fields to supply because the API is unauthenticated.

setupSteps.title: Respect the fair use policy
setupSteps.body: Per the PokeAPI docs, rate limiting has been removed but you are expected to limit request frequency and cache resources locally to keep hosting costs down. Repeated abuse can lead to a permanent IP ban.

sources.title: PokeAPI v2 documentation (authentication, fair use, base URL, /pokemon endpoint)
